title: redpanda console UI: crypto.randomUUID blocked on http origin (secure-context required)
description:
Schema create + likely other UI forms throw 'TypeError: crypto.randomUUID is not a function' on http://192.168.1.34:8082.

Root cause: NOT a Console bug, NOT an ACL issue. crypto.randomUUID (Web Crypto API) requires a Secure Context per W3C spec. Plain HTTP origins are insecure unless host is loopback (localhost/127.0.0.1). 192.168.1.34 over HTTP fails the check.

Confirmed 2026-04-29 after infra-wea (console now connects as admin, perms not the blocker).

Workarounds (immediate):
- SSH tunnel: ssh -L 8082:192.168.1.34:8082 agent@192.168.1.34 → http://localhost:8082 (loopback = secure context)
- Chrome flag: chrome://flags/#unsafely-treat-insecure-origin-as-secure add http://192.168.1.34:8082, relaunch

Proper fix: put Console behind TLS reverse proxy (Caddy or nginx) using internal CA cert (secrets/ca/infra-ca.crt). Same trust model as Schema Registry which is already HTTPS. Pattern reusable for any new browser UI on the homelab LAN.

References:
- https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Crypto/randomUUID
- https://w3c.github.io/webappsec-secure-contexts/
close_reason: Resolved by infra-d32 (Console TLS via Caddy). Verified 2026-04-30: https://192.168.1.34:8443 serves Console with cert chained to infra-CA, browser shows secure context, schema-create succeeds without crypto.randomUUID error. Plain :8082 closed. Smoke ALL PASS.

title: Fix broken otel-stack: collector DNS, ch-ui ARM mismatch, grafana port 3000 conflict
description:
Discovered during infra-64b (mem caps task) on 2026-04-22. Three otel-stack services are unhealthy on Pi and have been since 2026-04-21T21:44Z (before mem caps were added):

1. **otel-collector** — Crashlooping. DNS resolution of hostname 'clickhouse' fails: 'dial tcp: lookup clickhouse on 127.0.0.53:53: read: connection refused'. The collector runs in its own compose network but the service is defined at otel-stack/docker-compose.yml — clickhouse should resolve via the compose's internal DNS. Likely: collector started before clickhouse was ready, or the networks are not shared correctly, or the collector is hitting systemd-resolved on the host instead of the embedded DNS.

2. **otel-ch-ui** — Exec format error (ARM vs x86 mismatch). Image ghcr.io/caioricciuti/ch-ui:latest does not have a linux/arm64 variant (or the manifest is misconfigured). Options: switch to a known-ARM ch-ui image, build from source for arm64, or remove ch-ui from the stack.

3. **otel-grafana** — Never started since creation. Error: 'Bind for 0.0.0.0:3000 failed: port is already allocated'. Port 3000 is already bound by esb-gitea. otel-stack compose declares '3000:3000'; local-infra docs say Grafana should be at 3001. Need to remap Grafana host port to 3001 (or any free port) in otel-stack/docker-compose.yml.

Mem caps from infra-64b ARE correctly applied to all 5 containers — that's verified. This ticket is about restoring service, not mem sizing.
notes:
Fixed 2026-04-22.

Root causes:
- collector DNS: otel-collector/otel-grafana had NetworkSettings.Networks={} (orphaned from otel-stack_default after prior network-prune or partial recreate). Clean compose down --remove-orphans + up -d restored network attachment.
- ch-ui: upstream ghcr.io/caioricciuti/ch-ui:latest arm64 manifest ships x86 binary (exec format error). Dropped the service; ClickHouse Play UI at :8123/play covers the use-case.
- grafana port: remapped 3000->3001 (3000 held by esb-gitea). Matches local-infra docs.

Additional port conflicts discovered during deploy (not in original plan):
- clickhouse native 9000 collided with MinIO :9000 -> remapped to 9100:9000 (local-infra docs already canonical at 9100).
- collector self-metrics 8888 collided with configserver, then 8889 collided with pi-otel-agent host-net container. Removed host-port binding entirely; internal compose-network scrape via otel-collector:8888 still works.

Commits on master:
- f769c01 fix(otel-stack): remap grafana 3000->3001, drop broken ch-ui
- 0d76077 chore(otel-stack): remove stale loki/tempo/prometheus configs
- dbab7e4 chore(otel-stack): remove stale init.sh (MinIO bootstrap for removed tempo/loki)
- 02d04cd fix(otel-stack): remap clickhouse native 9000->9100 (MinIO conflict)
- d274384 fix(otel-stack): remap collector self-metrics 8888->8889 (configserver conflict)
- dbe201d fix(otel-stack): drop collector self-metrics host port (pi-otel-agent conflict)
- ce8c4e6 docs(plan): fix-otel-stack plan

Skill update (outside repo, ~/.claude/skills/ not a git repo):
- local-infra/SKILL.md: removed CH-UI section, updated collector self-metrics port note.

Verification passed:
- docker compose ps: all three services Up, clickhouse healthy.
- otel-collector NetworkSettings.Networks has otel-stack_default with IP 172.19.0.3 (no more {}).
- No "lookup clickhouse" DNS errors in collector logs.
- Health endpoint http://pi:13133/ returns {"status":"Server available"}.
- ClickHouse otel.otel_logs exists, OTLP/HTTP canary log ingested (count=1).
- Grafana http://pi:3001/api/health returns 200 {"database":"ok","version":"11.5.2"}.
- Gitea http://pi:3000 still returns 200 (no regression).
- grafana-clickhouse-datasource v4.15.0 plugin installed.

title: homelab TLS sweep: front all UIs with reverse proxy + internal CA
description:
Promote secrets/ca/infra-ca.crt (currently only mints Redpanda broker cert) to general homelab CA. Front all browser-facing UIs with HTTPS reverse proxy so Web Crypto APIs (crypto.randomUUID, SubtleCrypto, etc.) work without secure-context errors. Without this, every UI that adopts a new Web Crypto feature breaks on plain HTTP from non-localhost.

In-scope UIs:
- Redpanda Console (currently :8082, will be :8443 after infra-wea/0yi follow-up)
- Gitea (:3000)
- Grafana (:3001)
- MinIO console (likely :9001)
- LLDAP web UI (:17170)
- Inbucket UI (:9010)

Design questions to resolve before plan:
- Proxy choice: Caddy vs nginx vs Traefik
- DNS strategy: /etc/hosts entries (clients self-manage) vs DNSMasq on router (192.168.1.1) vs Pi-side dnsmasq
- Hostname scheme: <service>.home.lan vs <service>.lan vs subdomains of single name
- Cert lifecycle: how to mint per-service certs from infra-CA (script vs Caddy internal CA mode reusing the root)
- Trust distribution: install infra-CA on every workstation/laptop/phone that hits homelab UIs, or accept warnings on rare-use UIs

Architecture decision:
- Single proxy compose at proxy/ that routes by Host header to N backends across compose stacks (cross-network)
- Per-stack proxy sidecar (more containers, simpler networking)

Triggered by infra-0yi (Redpanda Console crypto.randomUUID failure on http://192.168.1.34:8082). MVP for Console only filed separately. This issue tracks the full-homelab pass.

title: redis: lock down default user once all clients on named auth
description:
After all consumers migrate (infra-b4i), restrict the default user so anonymous connections can no longer hit Redis.

Two options for the default user (pick one when picking this up):
- `user default off` — disables the user entirely. Cleanest. Anyone connecting without auth gets NOAUTH.
- `user default on >some-strong-password ~* &* +@all` — keeps it but forces auth. Slight footgun: any service still using the implicit no-auth path silently re-authenticates with stale config drift.

Steps:
1. Confirm `infra-b4i` is closed and zero clients connect anonymously (grep redis URIs across infra repo + app-config repo + service repos).
2. Edit redis/users.acl: change default line.
3. bash redis/smoke.sh — Step 3 of plan Task 5 (default-user sanity write/read) MUST now FAIL after change. Update smoke.sh accordingly: replace the "default still works" assertions with "default is disabled" assertions.
4. Update redis/README.md: scope row for default → `disabled` or `password-only`.
5. docker compose up -d redis on pi.
6. Run updated smoke.

This IS a breaking change. Coordinate with anyone running services against esb-redis.

Plan reference: docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-28-redis-acl-admin.md (Task 6 follow-up section).
notes:
Deployed 2026-04-28.
- Pre-deploy CLIENT LIST inventory: only docker healthcheck (172.18.0.1, redis-cli) hit default. No application-layer anonymous consumers.
- redis/users.acl: default line changed to `off`.
- docker-compose.yml redis.healthcheck.test: now `redis-cli --user admin --pass admin --no-auth-warning ping`.
- redis/smoke.sh: assertions flipped to expect NOAUTH from anonymous PING + WRONGPASS from AUTH default <anything>.
- redis/README.md: ACL users table marked default off; History section replaces Follow-up.

Verified post-deploy:
- bash redis/smoke.sh -> OK
- esb-redis healthcheck -> healthy under admin auth
- gitea API /version + /repos/admin/infra(/contents) -> 200
- CLIENT LIST grep "user=default" -> 0
- docker logs esb-redis | esb-gitea | grep WRONGPASS|NOAUTH|connection refused -> empty
close_reason: Closed

Docker Compose env_file does NOT feed Compose-time variable substitution — ${VAR} interpolation in docker-compose.yml requires VAR in the shell environment when running 'docker compose up'. env_file only injects vars into the *container* environment at runtime. For secret-file workflows where compose YAML uses ${SECRET}, wrap with: 'set -a; source secrets.env; set +a; docker compose up -d'. Discovered while designing redpanda restore (RP_BOOTSTRAP_USER pattern).
